history.text: It is located about 115 km southeast of Cape Town and is connected to the Mother City by the R43 highway (or coastal R44 scenic route) and N2 motorway.
1778 – 1837) was a Dutch teacher who arrived in Cape Town in 1815.

history.label: Growth
history.text: He was recruited by Dutch-speaking farmers who disliked that English was the only language used in all government schools.

history.label: Modern city
history.text: He settled in Caledon, but taught Dutch to farmers in a wide area around that town, including the Hemel-en-Aarde Valley.

history.label: Today
history.text: He often vacationed at the spring ("fontein") in present-day Hermanus, where he fished and grazed his sheep, the place eventually became known as "Hermanus Pieters se Fonteyn".

history.label: Civic role
history.text: The parents of Roger Bushell, leader of the "Great Escape" (the escape by Allied airmen from Stalag Luft III in World War II), retired in Hermanus after World War II, and are buried there.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Bushell's name is one of those commemorated on the war memorial. Location Hermanus lies along Walker Bay on the south coast of the Western Cape.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: It is also notable that Hermanus still boasts a historic railway station building without a railway line.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Sandbaai lies on the coast at the entrance to the Hemel-en-Aarde (Heaven and Earth) Valley.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Zwelihle, designated a "black" area by the apartheid era government, is a residential area that consists mainly of shacks.
